title: dryopteris squamiseta
intro: Dryopteris squamiseta, also known as the scaly shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The scaly shield fern contains various bioactive compounds with potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage, improve immune function, and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or extracts, and can be used externally as a poultice or applied topically to affected areas.
